item.title: Young Thug Didn’t Just Mutate Hip-Hop. He Changed the Whole Act of Vocalizing.
item.description: He single-handedly changed hip-hop into something futuristic and alien — right when the genre needed it most.

item.title: Diane Warren Is a Titan of Lavish Power Ballads. You Adore at Least One.
item.description: Her widescreen, acrobatic songs have dominated radios, karaoke nights and — most likely — a big portion of your memory.

item.title: Lucinda Williams’s Songs Can Turn Any Sentiment Into a Lit Fuse
item.description: Few artists sculpt songs with this level of power and emotional intensity.

item.title: Stevie Wonder Is the Ultimate Embodiment of American Music
item.description: These are the songs you’d play to newborns, or alien visitors, to demonstrate the purest joys of our musical tradition.

item.title: The Jay-Z Interview
item.description: The artist shares stories behind his famous songs, the writing challenges he sets for himself and how he has sustained a hip-hop career.

item.title: He Was Supposed to Die in Prison, but the Prosecutor Felt Guilty
item.description: Jessie Askew Jr. was sentenced to life without parole for a clumsy armed robbery with an unloaded gun. The man who sent him away was determined to bring him back home.

item.title: Takeaways From The Times’s Interview With Tucker Carlson
item.description: He spoke about the rupture with President Trump and what it portends for the conservative movement. Here are the key moments from our conversation.

item.title: What Does Tucker Carlson Really Believe? I Went to Maine to Find Out.
item.description: The conservative media commentator split with the administration over the war in Iran. Will the breakup last?

item.title: A Homeless Person’s Pet Needed Help. Should I Have Tried to Buy It?
item.description: I could tell that the man loved his cat, but also that he was unable to provide proper care for it.

item.title: How We Made the List of the 30 Greatest Living American Songwriters
item.description: First we polled hundreds of experts. Then Times critics spent weeks arguing about what constitutes greatness in American music.

item.title: The Right Successfully Coins a Lot of Insults. The Left Has ‘Chud.’
item.description: Sometimes it’s just a put-down. Sometimes it’s darkly ideological. That’s true of a lot of slang these days.

item.title: Jared Kushner, Steve Witkoff and the Profitable Business of Peace
item.description: The Trump administration is turning diplomacy into an asset to be leveraged.
